text:
Check out Sports IllustratedГўВҖВҷs Daily Rings, our daily Olympics podcast from Mitch Goldich and Dan Gartland. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts (Apple,Spotify) and find clips onSIГўВҖВҷs YouTube page.

Now that the U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs gymnastics team has made it through ThursdayГўВҖВҷs podium trainingГўВҖВ”the practice event at which, three years ago,Simone Biles first began to show signs of the vertigothat would derail her Tokyo OlympicsГўВҖВ”they will all sleep easier.ГӮ

Not just becauseBiles culminated the session with a breathtaking Yurchenko double pike vault, which suggests that the ГўВҖВңtwisties,ГўВҖВқ as the condition is known, is behind her. ThereГўВҖВҷs also a more elementary reason.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷre getting mattress toppers today,ГўВҖВқ says coach CГғВ©cile Landi. ГўВҖВңHallelujah!ГўВҖВқ

The 13 athletes (five womenГўВҖВҷs artistic gymnasts, five menГўВҖВҷs artistic gymnasts, two trampoline athletes and one rhythmic gymnast) arrived at the Olympic Village north of Paris on Monday. It took about 15 seconds for them to notice a problem: The mattresses felt like rocks.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңI sat on it and I was like, ГўВҖВҳOh my God,ГўВҖВҷ ГўВҖВқ says Landi with a laugh. ГўВҖВңI was like, ГўВҖВҳMary [McDaniel, who runs logistics]! Help!ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқГӮ

Help was already on the way. A few days earlier, the team had announced a partnership with BedJet, which makes a cooling system that attaches to the bed and also offers mattress toppers and sheets that wick away sweat, but the technological problems that swept the world last week delayed the itemsГўВҖВҷ arrival. The entire delegation expects to receive them Thursday.ГӮ

Coach Jess Graba lights up at the possibility. ГўВҖВңAre we getting any?ГўВҖВқ he asks. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm pretty sure the athletes will get those. IГўВҖВҷm not sure IГўВҖВҷm getting those. If I get one, IГўВҖВҷll be excited.ГўВҖВқ

The U.S. gymnastics team is just the latest squad to identify concerns about the accommodation at the Olympics, although many athletes admit itГўВҖВҷs been better than what they feared when they first learned of FranceГўВҖВҷs attempt to keep the enterprise as eco-friendly as possible by building beds from recycled cardboard and eschewing air conditioning. (Team USA immediately purchased portable air conditioning units and brought them to Paris.)ГӮ

The U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs water polo team recently received mattress toppers as well and the Australian womenГўВҖВҷs water polo team ordered their own mattress toppers, too.ГӮ

Many athletes have taken to social media to give tours of the furniture, with British diver Tom Daley bouncing on one bed and Irish rugby players Jordan Conroy and Harry McNulty flinging themselves onto another. So far, the cardboard seems to have held up.ГӮ

And the athletes are thrilled to have a real village experience, after a Tokyo Olympics marred by isolation. The U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs gymnastics team stayed in a hotel in an attempt to mitigate the risks; this time around, they are enjoying even such details as the option to grab a snack. ГўВҖВңThey were sliding it under the door for us [in Tokyo],ГўВҖВқ Graba recalls.ГӮ

Landi is a bit less effusive: ГўВҖВңThe food is what it is,ГўВҖВқ says Landi, who was born in Paris and grew up in Marseille. ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs not the French cuisine IГўВҖВҷm used to, but itГўВҖВҷs not that bad.ГўВҖВқ But she concedes that she too is enjoying the village, having already been starstruck by encounters with Spanish tennis player Rafael Nadal and American swimmer Katie Ledecky.

Indeed, everyone seems more relaxed than they did three years ago. The U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs gymnastics team declined to speak to the media but left the arena smiling and joking with one another. It will face its first test Sunday, in the qualifying round of the team event. But first: a Friday rest day. Literally.

Stephanie Apstein is a senior writer covering baseball and Olympic sports for Sports Illustrated, where she started as an intern in 2011 and has since covered a dozen World Series and three Olympics. She has twice won top honors from the Associated Press Sports Editors, and her work has been included in the Best American Sports Writing book series. She graduated from Trinity College with a bachelorГўВҖВҷs in French and Italian, and has a masterГўВҖВҷs in journalism from Columbia University.

title: U.S. Gymnastics Takes on Its First Olympic Opponent: Cardboard Beds
description: Eco-friendly lodging in the Olympic Village has been the talk of the Paris Games, but Team USA won't be losing any sleep over them thanks to a quick solution.
